stories.headline: Iran war live: Tehran insists on control of Hormuz amid reports of US talks
stories.summary: Washington and Tehran have agreed to stop attacks and renew talks in the Qatari capital, Doha, Axios reports.

stories.headline: US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
stories.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Kent on Reserve Bank of Australia's New Framework
stories.summary: Australian central bank Assistant Governor Chris Kent said the institution will be better prepared to respond to the next crisis it faces following a review of alternative monetary policy tools. In a speech in Sydney, Kent, who oversees financial markets at the Reserve Bank, said the cash rate target remains the primary and preferred instrument, but additional tools can play an important role during extraordinary times and provide some extra supp

stories.headline: Pakistan says its security forces killed 29 fighters along Afghan border
stories.summary: Strikes come a day after fighters armed with guns and explosives killed three soldiers in Karachi.

stories.headline: Philippines Sees Slower Growth, Weak Peso Beyond Marcos’ Term
stories.summary: The Philippines has cut its economic growth targets and sees a weaker peso beyond the end of the term of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. in 2028 amid headwinds such as the Middle East tensions and an intense El Niño weather event.

stories.headline: China: Tech Growth Distorts Energy Demand Forecasts
stories.summary: China faces greater uncertainty in forecasting energy demand as structural changes in the economy and the rapid expansion of new industries reshape consumption patterns, according to a top government official. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Praise pours in for ‘true Canadian heroes’ after last-minute World Cup win
stories.summary: From the prime minister to sport celebrities and fans on social media, Canadians have revelled in their team's win.

stories.headline: Iran threatens total halt to talks amid intensive US air activity near Hormuz
stories.summary: The IRGC said the weekend US strikes violated the framework deal and warned that violating vessels would face a "crushing response," as Euronews journalists in Doha observed US refuelling aircraft taking off towards Hormuz in the same formation as the previous night's strikes.

stories.summary: As Kyiv’s battlefield fortunes change, policies long favoured by the west could now do real damage to Moscow

stories.headline: Israel kills four Palestinians in Gaza as attacks on tents continue

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
stories.summary: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

stories.headline: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
stories.summary: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.

stories.headline: Three Firefighters Die as Wildfires Ravage Utah and Colorado
stories.summary: Fires are raging in the arid Southwest after a warm winter. Forecasters say the fires may grow rapidly, and parts of Colorado have been ordered to evacuate.

stories.headline: At Least 4 Dead in Kentucky Flooding, Governor Says
stories.summary: Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky said emergency crews rescued people from flooded homes and vehicles. Flash flood warnings continued on Sunday.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes days after peace agreement
stories.summary: Just days after signing a memorandum of understanding, the US and Iran have traded strikes.

stories.headline: More than 68,000 people still missing in Venezuela after devastating earthquakes
stories.summary: A preliminary estimate from the United Nations Development Programme put the cost of direct physical damage caused by the quakes at $6.7 billion.

stories.headline: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C
stories.summary: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.

stories.headline: Fourteen killed in Saudi Arabia helicopter crash
stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: AI ‘exuberance’ risks ending in lengthy investment bust, BIS warns
stories.summary: Weak returns could trigger a sharp pullback in funding for tech companies that threatens the global economy

stories.headline: Grim legacy of ‘Chavismo’ hinders Venezuela’s earthquake response
stories.summary: Socialist regime’s US-backed leader is facing a crisis as anger mounts over inadequate rescue efforts

stories.headline: Oil Pares Early Gains as US, Iran Halt Attacks After Flare-Up
stories.summary: Oil pared early gains after the US and Iran agreed to stop attacking each other, following flare-ups over the weekend that saw an oil supertanker hit near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: RBA Will Be Better Prepared to Handle Next Crisis, Kent Says
stories.summary: Australian central bank Assistant Governor Chris Kent said the institution will be better prepared to respond to the next crisis it faces following a review of alternative monetary policy tools.

stories.headline: Bond Heavyweights Target a Market Sweet Spot for New Warsh Era
stories.summary: Some of the biggest bond managers are zeroing in on one specific area of the market as the best place to ride out the early days of the Kevin Warsh era.

stories.headline: Big oil’s secretive trading arms are having an extraordinary year
stories.summary: Meet the corporate gamblers who never waste a good energy crisis

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquakes: death toll rises again to more than 1,400

Continue reading.

stories.headline: Protesters in Serbia doubt Vucic resignation will bring change
stories.summary: Thousands of protesters gathered in Serbia's Kraljevo, dismissing Aleksandar Vucic's pledge to resign.

stories.headline: Drone captures ongoing rescue efforts after Venezuela earthquakes
stories.summary: Drone footage from Venezuela's La Guaira shows widespread destruction after twin earthquakes devastated the region.

stories.headline: Nawrocki invites four presidents to informal summit amid dispute with Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki hosted the presidents of Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia and Romania for an informal meeting in Jurata ahead of the NATO summit in Ankara.

stories.headline: Second World War 'hell ship' found in Philippines with over 1,000 prisoners
stories.summary: Divers have found the wreck of the Hofuku Maru off Luzon in the Philippines. The Japanese freighter sank in 1944 with over 1,200 Allied POWs; a digitised military file finally solved the mystery.

stories.headline: Bond giant Pimco flexes muscles with private placements push
stories.summary: Firm tries to capitalise on some borrowers’ need to raise cash as boundaries with public markets blur

stories.headline: Apple to fight $500mn patent bill at UK Supreme Court
stories.summary: Battle over licensing of mobile connection protocols could shape global rates for other technologies

stories.headline: Sovereign Funds Pivot Further to Private Assets in Risky Markets
stories.summary: The world’s biggest public investors plan to shift more capital into private and less liquid assets amid rising risks to their traditional bond-and-stock portfolios, according to an industry survey.

stories.headline: Wall Street Is Abandoning Bets on a Stronger Euro
stories.summary: Wall Street banks are capitulating on bets for a stronger euro, as markets see the US outpacing Europe on interest-rate hikes for the rest of this year.

stories.headline: Prabowo Risks Prompt Global Banks to Pull Cash Out of Indonesia
stories.summary: The three biggest foreign banks in Indonesia have shipped around $640 million of their earnings out of Southeast Asia’s largest economy since 2024 as they pare exposure amid President Prabowo Subianto’s increasingly state-focused economic policies.

stories.headline: BlueBay Sees Near-Term Risk in Japan AI Stocks Followed by Rally
stories.summary: RBC BlueBay Asset Management remains bullish on Japanese AI-related stocks, expecting the rally to extend into 2027, while trimming near-term risk ahead of a potential slowdown in July and August.

stories.headline: Private Credit Quietly Backs the Craze Propping Up US Consumers
stories.summary: Private credit companies are increasingly backing the "buy now, pay later" craze that's propping up US consumers. This trend is throwing up red flags for credit raters, former regulatory chiefs and others on guard for potential risks. René Ismail explains  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Williams Said to Near $5.5 Billion Deal for Momentum Midstream
stories.summary: Williams Cos. is in advanced talks to acquire rival natural gas pipeline operator Momentum Midstream for about $5.5 billion in what would be one of its largest deals ever, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/28/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Center for Strategic & International Studies Senior Advisor and Fmr. Vice Foreign Minister of Panama Carlos Ruiz-Hernandez, Fmr. stories.headline: Aramco Helicopter Crash in Ras Tanura Kills All 14 on Board
stories.summary: A helicopter operated by Saudi Aramco crashed Sunday in the energy heartland of Ras Tanura, killing all 14 passengers on board.

stories.headline: Can USMCA Survive Trump’s Second Term?
stories.summary: As Canada, Mexico, and the United States begin reviewing the USMCA trade pact, former diplomats and trade negotiators say the stakes extend far beyond tariffs. Former Canadian Ambassador Kirsten Hillman, former Mexican Ambassador Gerónimo Gutiérrez, and former US trade counsel Kelly Ann Shaw argue that the agreement now underpins everything from supply chains and agriculture to intellectual property and investment. stories.headline: Leverage That Fueled US Stock Rally Becomes a Growing Concern
stories.summary: The leverage that helped fuel the US stock rally is now becoming an increasing source of unease.

stories.headline: Colorado’s Primary for Governor Pivots to Trump and Who Will Fight Him
stories.summary: The Democratic primary on Tuesday is likely to decide the next governor, and Senator Michael Bennet, once seen as a shoo-in, is in a dogfight with Attorney General Phil Weiser.

stories.headline: Swiss glaciers melting at alarming rate in June as Europe faces extreme heat
stories.summary: All snow and ice accumulated over the past winter is expected to have melted by Monday. Over the past century, this tipping point usually only arrives in mid-August on average.

stories.headline: Iraqi political officials arrested on corruption charges in overnight raid
stories.summary: Iraqi security forces sealed off all entrances to the capital’s heavily fortified Green Zone early Sunday and carried out raids inside the compound that houses key government institutions and foreign embassies.

stories.headline: Warsh Makes Debut on the Global Stage With the Class of 2008

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.headline: White House Releases Images of the Trump ‘Patriot Passport’
stories.summary: The State Department said it planned to release 40,000 U.S. passports featuring an image of the president to commemorate the United States’ 250th anniversary.

stories.headline: Trump reveals new images of passport with his portrait to mark America’s 250th
stories.summary: The State Department said the new document “boldly honors” the president and the nation’s founding. The rendering differed from previous images of the passport in several ways.

stories.headline: Budapest's first Pride since Orban left power
stories.summary: Budapest held its first Pride march since the end of former prime minister Viktor Orban's 16-year government with thousands turning out to celebrate.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline
stories.summary: Very few North Koreans know about her, as her origin could threaten the regime’s legitimacy.

stories.headline: Watch: A race against the clock for Venezuela earthquake rescuers
stories.summary: There is a sense that the next few hours will be critical for saving anyone who is still trapped, says the BBC's Dan Johnson reporting from the border of Venezuela and Columbia.

stories.headline: GOP candidate with same name as senator can stay on Alaska ballot, judge rules
stories.summary: Supporters of incumbent Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan say a challenger with the same name is not running in “good faith,” but a judge says that is not an issue.

stories.headline: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
stories.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

stories.headline: Rainbow flags await Egypt and Iran at awkward Pride Match
stories.summary: Iran's coach refuses to discuss pride celebrations in Seattle, where his country face Egypt in a crucial Group G match.

stories.summary: Bonded labour was outlawed in India in 1975, but it still continues in villages and small towns.

stories.headline: Sydney woman wakes from induced coma more than a week after shark attack
